articles.title: OpenClaw and Claude can put your AI-generated podcasts in Spotify
articles.summary: Spotify launches a command-line tool enabling AI agents like Claude and OpenClaw to generate and publish AI-created podcasts directly to the platform alongside traditional shows.

articles.title: Google’s taking a big swing at AI health with the Fitbit Air
articles.summary: Google launches Fitbit Air, a $99 screenless fitness band with metallic fabric clasp, positioning it as an affordable health tracking alternative. The device returns to Fitbit's roots before the company shifted focus to smartwatches.

articles.title: Musk’s biggest loyalist became his biggest liability
articles.summary: Shivon Zilis, Musk's loyal adviser and mother of four of his children, testified in the Musk v. Altman trial about her role managing his AI portfolio across Tesla, Neuralink, and OpenAI since 2017.

articles.title: Aurora’s Chris Urmson on why self-driving trucks are finally ready to scale
articles.summary: Aurora's self-driving trucks are scaling commercially after starting operations last April, moving from handful of vehicles to hundreds this year. CEO Chris Urmson believes the technology is finally ready for large-scale deployment.

articles.title: 2 days left: Get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
articles.summary: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 offers 50% off a second pass with savings up to $410. The promotion ends May 8 at 11:59 p.m. PT.

articles.title: China’s Moonshot AI raises $2B at $20B valuation as demand for open-source AI skyrockets
articles.summary: China's Moonshot AI raised $2B at $20B valuation, with $200M annualized recurring revenue in April. The company benefits from surging demand for open-source AI solutions.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud by addressing legacy platform limitations.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-200 monthly pricing has frustrated developers, prompting interest in Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block that offers similar autonomous coding capabilities without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt decoded to a puzzle about Berlin nightclub entry logic, attracting thousands of engineer applicants.

articles.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
articles.summary: Terminal windows have become increasingly important in modern computing, contrary to predictions that graphical interfaces would eliminate command-line tools. The command line remains superior for many tasks despite the dominance of GUI-based systems.

articles.title: Widely used Daemon Tools disk app backdoored in monthlong supply-chain attack
articles.summary: Daemon Tools disk mounting app was backdoored for a month via supply-chain attack, with malicious updates delivered through official developer servers affecting Windows versions 12.5.0.2421-12.5.0.2434.

articles.title: Why Reddit blocked my daily visit to its mobile website
articles.summary: Reddit blocked a user's mobile web access with an overlay forcing them to download the app. No skip option or alternatives were provided.

articles.title: The Download: the tech reshaping IVF and the rise of balcony solar
articles.summary: New technologies including AI, robotics, and genetic editing are transforming IVF to make it faster, more effective, and affordable. These innovations promise better outcomes but raise ethical concerns about accessibility and genetic modification.

articles.title: The balcony solar boom is coming to the US
articles.summary: US states are considering legislation to permit plug-in balcony solar systems that require minimal installation. Though popular in Europe, experts raise safety concerns about integration with existing home electrical systems.

articles.title: What’s next for IVF
articles.summary: IVF has helped millions conceive over 48 years, but success rates are declining. The procedure remains slow, painful, expensive, and inaccessible to many despite technological advances.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM upgrades from V0 to V1, prioritizing correctness in reinforcement learning before making corrections. The update emphasizes accuracy and reliability improvements.

articles.title: Adding Benchmaxxer Repellant to the Open ASR Leaderboard
articles.summary: The Open ASR Leaderboard has added Benchmaxxer Repellant to prevent gaming of rankings. This measure aims to ensure fair evaluation and authentic performance metrics for speech recognition systems.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent an advanced iteration of large language models, focusing on improved architectural design and training methodologies. The article explores the technical foundations and construction techniques behind these enhanced models.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation. The conversation explores upcoming trends and advancements in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and management. These AI solutions enable companies to work more efficiently while maintaining imaginative output.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools freely available to developers and farmers for sustainable farming innovation.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google's AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features provide top gardening tips to help plants thrive. Users can leverage these tools for gardening advice and plant care guidance.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners for a $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in April 2026, showcasing new developments in artificial intelligence technology and capabilities.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare by exploring new models for AI-assisted medical care. This aims to enhance clinical decision-making and patient outcomes.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using frontier AI models. The collaboration aims to leverage advanced AI technology for research advancement.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new approach for resilient distributed AI training. The method improves fault tolerance and efficiency in large-scale model training across multiple systems.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, accusing them of stealing a charity. The trial revealed evidence of settlement discussions and allegations that xAI used OpenAI's models for training.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: The podcast discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with coding improvements and higher pricing, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #242 discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's improved ChatGPT image model with enhanced text accuracy, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6. The episode covers recent breakthroughs in multimodal AI and Chinese language models.

articles.title: AI helping ease the UK’s NHS burden
articles.summary: AI-enabled virtual care is being deployed in the UK NHS to help manage patient overflow and reduce the 7.25 million waiting list by shifting care from hospitals to community settings.

articles.title: HP and the art of AI and data for the enterprise
articles.summary: HP discusses enterprise AI challenges, including data preparation for AI ingestion and deciding between cloud versus local compute solutions. The company emphasizes that leveraging first-party data effectively at enterprise scale requires proper data management infrastructure.

articles.title: US government increases AI suppliers and rethinks Anthropic’s role
articles.summary: The US Pentagon expanded its AI supplier roster to include Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, and Reflection AI for classified operations. Anthropic faced contract cancellation after refusing unrestricted government use of its technology.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture that addresses key robot navigation challenges by answering where robots are, where they're going, and how to get there in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers unveiled SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention enables LLMs to process 100 million token context windows while maintaining accuracy, significantly expanding model memory capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants inadvertently leak sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries, posing security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, highlighting supply chain security risks.

articles.title: Meta AI Releases NeuralBench: A Unified Open-Source Framework to Benchmark NeuroAI Models Across 36 EEG Tasks and 94 Datasets
articles.summary: Meta AI released NeuralBench, an open-source framework standardizing evaluation of AI models trained on brain signals across 36 EEG tasks and 94 datasets, addressing inconsistencies in neuroimaging research.

articles.title: OpenAI Introduces MRC (Multipath Reliable Connection): A New Open Networking Protocol for Large-Scale AI Supercomputer Training Clusters
articles.summary: OpenAI introduced MRC, a new networking protocol for AI supercomputer training clusters developed with AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft, and NVIDIA. The protocol addresses networking bottlenecks where delayed data transfers cause GPU idle time during large model training.

articles.title: Zyphra Releases ZAYA1-8B: A Reasoning MoE Trained on AMD Hardware That Punches Far Above Its Weight Class
articles.summary: Zyphra released ZAYA1-8B, a small 8B parameter MoE model trained on AMD hardware that outperforms much larger models on math and coding benchmarks using novel Markovian RSA methodology.

articles.title: Whoop vs. Fitbit Air: I compared Google's new fitness band to the industry favorite
articles.summary: Google launched the Fitbit Air, a screenless fitness band competing with Whoop. This comparison examines specs to determine which wearable is superior.

articles.title: 10 secret Netflix codes I use to find hidden movies (and how to enter them) - it's easy
articles.summary: Netflix has secret codes that unlock hidden movies in niche genres and micro-categories at no extra cost. Learn how to use these codes to discover buried content.

articles.title: Best travel VPNs of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
articles.summary: This article reviews the best travel VPNs for 2026, helping travelers access their favorite shows while abroad by bypassing geographical restrictions.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Tool Calling in AI Agents
articles.summary: Article discusses strategies for effectively implementing tool calling capabilities in AI agents, enabling them to interact with external systems and perform complex tasks.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: Non-deterministic agents produce different outputs from identical inputs across runs. The article discusses implementing statistical guardrails to ensure reliable and consistent behavior in such systems.

articles.title: Agentic RAG Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Agentic RAG combines retrieval-augmented generation with autonomous agents, enabling AI systems to dynamically retrieve and process information. The article explains this technology across three difficulty levels for various audiences.

articles.title: How Major Reasoning Models Converge to the Same “Brain” as They Model Reality Increasingly Better
articles.summary: Major reasoning models converge toward similar internal structures as they improve at modeling reality, suggesting a universal optimal brain architecture exists for understanding the world.

articles.title: I Rewrote a Real Data Workflow in Polars. Pandas Didn’t Stand a Chance.
articles.summary: A developer rewrote a pandas data workflow using Polars, achieving dramatic performance improvement from 61 seconds to 0.20 seconds while discovering unexpected mental model shifts.

articles.title: When the Uncertainty Is Bigger Than the Shock: Scenario Modelling for English Local Elections
articles.summary: A scenario analysis case study examining calibrated uncertainty and historical error in English local election forecasting, demonstrating when models are most valuable by refusing to predict.

articles.title: Linked and Loaded: Gaijin Single Sign-On Now Available on GeForce NOW
articles.summary: GeForce NOW now supports Gaijin single sign-on, allowing faster logins for cloud gaming. Seven new games were added to the platform this week, enhancing instant access across devices.

articles.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI, Now With MRC
articles.summary: NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet fabric is advanced AI networking technology deployed by OpenAI, Microsoft, and Oracle for building powerful AI factories with superior performance and scale.

articles.title: NVIDIA and ServiceNow Partner on New Autonomous AI Agents for Enterprises
articles.summary: NVIDIA and ServiceNow partner to develop autonomous AI agents for enterprises that can safely operate across real workflows with context and control, moving beyond basic AI generation and reasoning.

articles.title: Agents that transact: Introducing Amazon Bedrock AgentCore payments, built with Coinbase and Stripe
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock introduces AgentCore payments with Coinbase and Stripe, enabling AI agents to autonomously transact and pay for services. This marks a shift toward an agentic economy where agents discover, evaluate, and purchase resources in real-time.

articles.title: Cost effective deployment of vision-language models for pet behavior detection on AWS Inferentia2
articles.summary: Furbo Pet Camera uses vision-language models on AWS Inferentia2 chips for cost-effective pet behavior detection. The shift from GPU to Inferentia2 reduced inference costs while maintaining real-time alert accuracy.

articles.title: How Hapag-Lloyd uses Amazon Bedrock to transform customer feedback into actionable insights
articles.summary: Hapag-Lloyd, a global shipping company, uses Amazon Bedrock to analyze customer feedback and extract actionable insights, enhancing their digital customer experience across 140 countries.

articles.title: How to Build In-Vehicle AI Agents with NVIDIA: From Cloud to Car
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables developers to build AI agents for vehicles that move beyond simple command-response systems to intelligent, multimodal AI capable of reasoning and planning in automotive cockpits.

articles.title: Building for the Rising Complexity of Agentic Systems with Extreme Co-Design
articles.summary: AI is shifting from simple request-response models to complex agentic systems that autonomously call tools, spawn sub-agents, and manage their own context. This requires new co-design approaches to handle increased complexity.

articles.title: Optimize Supply Chain Decision Systems Using NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills
articles.summary: NVIDIA's cuOpt Agent Skills optimizes supply chain decisions by automating complex operations research problems traditionally requiring specialized teams. The solution addresses fluctuating demand, volatile costs, and capacity constraints through agentic AI.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon footprint mitigation technology and making it publicly available.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: A person with an unconventional background created innovative XR technology by applying unique perspectives and skills from outside the traditional tech industry.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient shaping for high-dimensional vision.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers propose methods for identifying interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. This work analyzes LLMs through feature attribution and interaction analysis to make AI decision-making more trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven methods for designing imaging systems that optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. An encoder maps objects to images, which are corrupted by noise, with an information estimator quantifying measurement quality.
